steps.title: Fastest check: compare a table or graph
steps.content: For an equivalent-expression question, enter the original expression and the answer choices in a calculator table or graph. The correct choice must match for several inputs, not just one. The matching choice is C, $3w^2 + 11w - 70$.

steps.title: Algebra check
steps.content: Use the work below to confirm the identity for every allowed input. This matters because one random matching input is only a quick screen, not a proof that two expressions are always equivalent.

steps.title: Combine like terms
steps.content: The middle terms combine to $-10w+21w=11w$, so the product is $$3w^2+11w-70.$$ That confirms C is correct. This also checks the answer choices: C is the only option with all three parts, $3w^2$, $+11w$, and $-70$.
